title: Laftagareen Re-Elected Southwest State President as Constitutional Standoff Deepens
detail: Southwest State's parliament in Baidoa re-elected Abdiaziz Hassan Mohamed Laftagareen as state president by a vote of 66 to 13 in a fast-tracked process, consolidating his legitimacy as the standoff with Mogadishu intensifies. Laftagareen had suspended relations with the Federal Government on March 17 and resigned from President Mohamud's JSP party on March 18, citing the unconstitutional March 4 passage of constitutional amendments by a boycotted federal parliament. His re-election signals that Southwest State's parliament and political establishment remain unified behind the state's defiance of federal overreach. Puntland and Jubbaland have also maintained parallel standoffs with Mogadishu. The re-election effectively cements a de facto tripartite opposition — Southwest State, Puntland, and Jubbaland — to Mohamud's constitutional agenda, creating the most serious federal-state crisis since 2021.

title: SNA and AUSSOM Kill 30+ Al-Shabaab Fighters in Bulunagaad Joint Operation
detail: Somalia National Army forces and AUSSOM troops (including UPDF Battle Group 44) conducted a joint ground-and-air operation near Bulunagaad Base in Lower Shabelle, killing more than 30 al-Shabaab militants. The operation was part of Operation Rolling Thunder / Operation Bader — the joint campaign targeting al-Shabaab supply routes and staging areas in Lower Shabelle that began on February 28. In a separate simultaneous operation, SNA forces in Middle Shabelle prevented a suspected suicide bombing targeting military bases, neutralizing the threat before detonation. The Bulunagaad area is strategically significant as a historical al-Shabaab logistics hub for attacks on Mogadishu from the south and southwest.
